description_long: This is a combination wrench featuring a closed, ring-shaped end with internal teeth on one side and an open, U-shaped jaw on the other. The body is elongated and slightly tapered, made from a single piece of dark, matte-finished metal, likely steel. The ring end is designed to fit hexagonal or square nuts and bolts, providing a secure grip, while the open end allows for quick placement and removal. The surface appears smooth with no visible grip pattern or rubber inserts, and the tool is free from rust or wear marks. There are no visible brand logos or manufacturer markings on the shaft or heads. This type of wrench is commonly found in garages, workshops, and urban maintenance settings for tightening or loosening fasteners.
description: A dark, matte-finished combination wrench with a ring end and an open jaw end, made of smooth metal and free of markings or wear.
description_view_0: The wrench is seen from above, clearly showing both the open jaw and the toothed ring end, as well as the smooth, unmarked shaft.
description_view_1: A side profile of the wrench, highlighting its thin, flat body and the depth of the ring end.
description_view_2: The wrench is viewed from the opposite side, again displaying both the open and ring ends, with the shaft's flatness visible.
description_view_3: An angled view showing the thickness of the wrench and the contours of both ends, emphasizing the solid metal construction.
